channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: KPLC announces planned power interruptions in Nairobi and four other counties for maintenance on May 13
item.description: Kenya Power and Lighting Company (KPLC) has announced scheduled electricity interruptions affecting several parts of the country on Wednesday, May 13, as part of routine system maintenance. The planne...

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Photos and Stories from George Clooney’s Work on The Peacemaker (1997)
item.description: This article presents a collection of behind-the-scenes photographs from the 1997 film The Peacemaker, offering a glimpse into George Clooney’s early years as a rising Hollywood star. At the time, Clo...

item.title: Treasury models extreme $200 oil price scenario amid Iran conflict risks and global supply shocks
item.description: The article examines a striking scenario outlined in Australia’s federal budget papers, in which Treasury has modelled an extreme global energy shock that pushes oil prices up to US$200 per barrel. Th...

item.title: Duterte Plans to Appoint Peter Haynes as Lawyer for ICC Case
item.description: Former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte is planning to appoint Peter Haynes, an expert in international criminal and humanitarian law, as his new lawyer for the crimes against humanity case at the...

item.title: Yoweri Museveni Begins Seventh Term As Uganda’s President Amid Controversy
item.description: Ugandan President Yoweri Museveni don start him seventh term after nearly 40 years for power. The inauguration happen for Kololo Independence Grounds, Kampala, with plenty supporters dey witness the c...

item.title: Canada Reminds Households of Census Requirements and Potential Fines
item.description: May 12, 2026, marks Census Day in Canada, prompting households to submit their census questionnaires. The Census of Population occurs every five years under the Statistics Act, and participation is ma...

item.title: Anastasia Sierra Explores Motherhood, Aging, and Memory in 'The Witching Hour'
item.description: This interview features photographer Anastasia Sierra discussing her ongoing project 'The Witching Hour,' a cinematic and emotionally layered photographic series that explores motherhood, aging, and f...

item.title: Multiple Ministers Resign Amid Growing Pressure on Keir Starmer's Leadership
item.description: Keir Starmer's premiership faces a serious crisis as a fourth minister, Health Minister Zubir Ahmed, resigns from the Government, joining others including Jess Phillips, Alex Davies-Jones, and Miatta ...

item.title: The Age Superquiz for Wednesday 13 May with Mini Crossword Details
item.description: This article from The Age presents its regular Superquiz feature for Wednesday, 13 May, offering readers a chance to engage with a daily general knowledge challenge. The Superquiz is part of the publi...

item.title: Police Sergeant Killed, Two Officers Wounded in Anti-Narcotics Operation in La Castellana, Negros Occidental
item.description: A police sergeant was killed while two other officers were injured during an anti-narcotics operation in La Castellana, Negros Occidental on Monday. M/Sgt. Davy Carampatana died from multiple stab wou...

item.title: Tanzania Completes Drilling of 12 Wells in Songo Songo Gas Block
item.description: In Dodoma, Tanzania's Deputy Minister for Energy, Salome Makamba, announced that 12 wells have been successfully drilled at the Songo Songo natural gas block. The Tanzanian government through the Tanz...

item.title: Rising Interest in Electric Vehicles Driven by High Gas Prices and Consumer Benefits
item.description: Electric vehicles (EVs) are experiencing renewed interest in several markets following recent surges in gas prices linked to global events, including the onset of the Iran war. The author, a four-year...

item.title: US Senate moves to confirm Frank Garcia to lead State Department Africa Bureau amid diplomatic staffing shortages
item.description: The US Senate is advancing the confirmation of Frank Garcia as the new assistant secretary for the State Department’s Africa Bureau, marking the first Senate-confirmed leadership of the bureau since t...

item.title: Keir Starmer Maintains Authority Amid Leadership Tensions in Labour Party
item.description: During the latest weekly cabinet meeting, Prime Minister Keir Starmer delivered a pointed message directed at Health Secretary Wes Streeting, who has publicly indicated he is prepared for a leadership...

item.title: Report Highlights Challenges Boys Face in Quebec Schools from Kindergarten Onwards
item.description: A recent report from Quebec highlights that boys are consistently falling behind girls in the education system, with differences appearing as early as kindergarten. The study, prepared by Quebec Solid...

item.title: PMDC mandates MDCAT and registration for students planning medical studies abroad
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The Pakistan Medical and Dental Council (PMDC) has clarified that students planning to pursue medical or dental education abroad must first qualify for the MDCAT examination and complete th...

item.title: Trump Posts Video Criticizing Michelle Obama While Highlighting JD Vance
item.description: On May 12, 2026, former President Donald Trump shared a video on his Truth Social platform that directly criticized former First Lady Michelle Obama while praising Vice President JD Vance. The video c...

item.title: Overview of the federal budget: continued deficits amid weaker economic outlook
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers’ fifth federal budget presents a mixed fiscal picture, with a slight improvement in the government’s overall budget position but a notably weaker economic outlook. According to ...

item.title: Fatal runway intrusion at Denver International Airport linked to suicide, officials confirm
item.description: A tragic incident occurred at Denver International Airport when a man was struck and killed by a Frontier Airlines aircraft during its takeoff roll. Authorities have confirmed that the individual, ide...

item.title: Army Foils Infiltration Attempt on LoC in Poonch, One Intruder Killed
item.description: On Tuesday, the Indian Army successfully foiled an infiltration attempt along the Line of Control (LoC) in the Krishna Ghati sector of Poonch district, located in the Pir Panjal Valley of Jammu and Ka...
